channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Ten Commandments, ‘In God We Trust’ in classrooms is now Arkansas law
item.description: Arkansas Gov. Sarah Huckabee Sanders signed a bill into law this week making religious displays in classrooms state law.
item.dc:creator: Alex Kienlen, Fox 5

item.title: Republicans deny Democrats' requests for El Salvador oversight trips
item.description: House Republican committee chairs are denying Democrats’ requests to travel to El Salvador and visit its CECOT prison facility as scrutiny builds on the Trump administration’s handling of Kilmar Abrego Garcia’s mistaken deportation.
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, NewsNation

item.title: Trump moves to invoke Schedule F to make it easier to fire some federal workers
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump has begun making one of the controversial personnel changes for government employees that was spelled out in the conservative Project 2025 blueprint for his second term.
item.dc:creator: CHRIS MEGERIAN and WILL WEISSERT, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Anti-Israel Harvard students conspire to smear law firms critical of campus antisemitism: report
item.description: Anti-Israel Harvard Law students organized a smear campaign against law firms that have stood up against campus antisemitism, according to a Washington Free Beacon investigation.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Judge blocks Trump administration’s from passport changes affecting transgender Americans
item.description: BOSTON (AP) — A federal judge on Friday blocked the Trump administration from enacting a policy that bans the use of “X” marker used by many nonbinary people on passports as well as the changing of gender markers.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL CASEY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Judge says detained Tufts student must be transferred from Louisiana to Vermont
item.description: A federal judge has ordered that a Tufts University student from Turkey who was detained by immigration authorities in March be brought to Vermont.
item.dc:creator: Holly Ramer, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Trump dishes on Abrego-Garcia's 'unbelievably bad' record and Democrat's 'fake' El Salvador trip
item.description: President Donald Trump said that Kilmar Abrego Garcia holds an “unbelievably bad" record, following Sen. Chris Van Hollen’s Thursday visit to El Salvador to meet him.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Federal judge orders halt to Trump admin's CFPB terminations
item.description: A federal judge ordered the Trump administration to temporarily halt mass layoffs at the Consumer Financial Protections Bureau after an appeals court had previously narrowed an earlier injunction.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump says Ukraine-Russia talks 'coming to a head' and 'no one is playing' him in push to end war
item.description: PARIS (AP) — President Donald Trump on Friday said negotiations between Ukraine and Russia are “coming to a head” and insisted that neither side is “playing” him in his push to end the grinding war. Trump spoke shortly after Secretary of State Marco Rubio warned that the U.S.
item.dc:creator: ANGELA CHARLTON and HANNA ARHIROVA, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: California to sue Trump administration over AmeriCorps cuts
item.description: Gov. Gavin Newsom announced late Thursday that California will sue the Trump administration over the Department of Government Efficiency’s cuts to AmeriCorps.
item.dc:creator: Iman Palm, Fox 5

item.title: US senator returns from El Salvador trip, says Abrego Garcia case is about far more than one man
item.description: Maryland Sen. Chris Van Hollen says Kilmar Abrego Garcia, who was wrongly deported, told him he has been moved from the notorious Salvadoran prison known as CECOT to a detention center with better conditions.
item.dc:creator: Mary Clare Jalonick, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: The Latest: Class action lawsuit challenges Trump’s crackdown on student visas
item.description: A federal judge who blocked Donald Trump’s administration from dismantling the Consumer Financial Protection Bureau has ruled that the agency can’t go forward immediately with plans to fire hundreds of employees.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Maryland Sen. Van Hollen meets with Abrego Garcia in El Salvador amid court fight over US return
item.description: SAN SALVADOR (AP) — Maryland Sen. Chris Van Hollen met with Kilmar Abrego Garcia on Thursday evening in El Salvador, coming face to face with the wrongly deported man after two days in the country pushing for his release.
item.dc:creator: YOLANDA MAGAÑA and MARY CLARE JALONICK, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: US citizen held by ICE despite judge seeing birth certificate
item.description: Juan Carlos Lopez-Gomez, who was born in the United States, was detained Wednesday in Florida by the state’s highway patrol and was charged with illegally entering the Sunshine State as an “unauthorized alien” under a state law that has been temporarily blocked by a judge, the Florida Phoenix news o
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump has privately discussed firing Jerome Powell for months: Source
item.description: President Trump has privately discussed firing Jerome Powell for several months, a source confirms to NewsNation.
item.dc:creator: Kevin Bohn, NewsNation

item.title: Nearly 90% of Consumer Financial Protection Bureau cut as Trump's government downsizing continues
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump is drastically shrinking the workforce and mission of the Consumer Financial Protection Bureau, eviscerating an agency created after the Great Recession with the goal of protecting Americans from fraud, abuse and deceptive practices.
item.dc:creator: CHRIS MEGERIAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: AP and the Trump administration argue over presidential access before appeals judges
item.description: The Associated Press and the Trump administration returned to a courtroom Thursday — and will be in another on Friday — as part of the high-stakes battle over who can control which journalists are able to question the president. Lawyers argued before a three-judge panel of the U.S.
item.dc:creator: DAVID BAUDER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Barack Obama, Bernie Sanders, Ben Carson among most liked politicians, surveys show
item.description: In one YouGov survey on Democratic figures, 58 percent of respondents said they felt “very favorable” or “somewhat favorable” toward Obama.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, NewsNation

item.title: GOP lawmakers investigate nonprofit founded by Casey DeSantis; executive director resigns
item.description: The program has been under scrutiny by Republicans in the Florida House, at the same time as the first lady considers a run for governor.
item.dc:creator: Ty Russell, WFLA, NBC

item.title: IRS asked to revoke Harvard's tax-exempt status
item.description: Only the IRS can review and revoke the existing status of taxpaying entities and has traditionally done so without presidential interference, as mandated by tax laws.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, NewsNation

item.title: New York AG referred to DOJ for alleged mortgage fraud
item.description: If proven, the claims could lead to prosecution for one of New York’s highest-ranking officials, who has long argued that “no one is above the law.
item.dc:creator: Johan Sheridan, NewsNation

item.title: White House invites Rachel Morin's mother to press briefing to share daughter's story
item.description: The mother of a Maryland woman who was killed by an individual who entered the country illegally addressed reporters at the White House on Wednesday as the Trump administration seeks to go on offense over the case of an individual who was mistakenly deported to El Salvador.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, NewsNation

item.title: Judge: 'Probable cause' to hold U.S. in contempt over Alien Enemies Act deportations
item.description: The government sent several planeloads of alleged gang members to El Salvador, including 137 people under the act, the White House said at the time. The judge gave the U.S. until April 23 to respond.
item.dc:creator: NPR Staff, MPR News

item.title: Funding for NYC Pre-K and 3-K permanently locked in: Mayor
item.description: NEW YORK CITY (PIX11) -- For the first time ever, New York City has permanently locked in funding for its Pre-K and 3-K programming, Mayor Eric Adams announced on Wednesday morning.
item.dc:creator: Dominique Jack, PIX 11

item.title: Lawmakers press Hope Florida officials over political contributions
item.description: Lawmakers questioned Hope Florida officials Tuesday morning to further investigate those involved in the ongoing probe into the non-profit and a mysterious money trail tied to a $10 million Medicaid settlement.
item.dc:creator: Mackenzie LaPorte, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Trump plans order to cut funding for NPR and PBS
item.description: President Trump and GOP members of Congress have accused the public broadcasters of biased and "woke" programming. The president plans a rescission, which would give Congress 45 days to approve the directive or allow funding to be restored.
item.dc:creator: David Folkenflik and Scott Neuman, MPR News

item.title: Getting the IRS on the phone is more difficult this tax filing season, experts say
item.description: Taxpayers calling the IRS for help processing their taxes this filing season may find it harder than normal to get someone on the phone.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Minneapolis council member Emily Koski drops out of mayor's race
item.description: Minneapolis City Council member Emily Koski has dropped out of the mayor’s race, according to a campaign update Monday morning.
item.dc:creator: Cari Spencer, MPR News
